forecaster_note: Small to tiny but clean, focused on the south-exposed corners — pretty much flat everywhere else. Just traces of mid-to-longer-period S swell in the water with breezy offshore winds, so little peelers at the south magnets (Long Reef, Curl Curl) and a paddle on a floaty board if you're keen. Tuesday's much the same: tiny but clean. INCOMING: a southerly change Wednesday brings wind and building S/SE swell, then a solid E groundswell fills in for the WEEKEND — Sunday the standout at chest-head-plus with light winds. — Hugh McDowell (Surfline)

wind.summary: WNW 17kph, gusting 36 — straight offshore at all four spots all day. Clean and groomed, just a punchy gust or two on the paddle-out.

shark.summary: Three SLS drone sightings in the last 24h, all NORTH of your spots — Palm Beach (7:28am Sun), Mona Vale (1:52pm Sun) & Warriewood (2:27pm Sun), water evacuated at each. No detections at Dee Why, Long Reef, Curl Curl or Manly, but the northern beaches are active — stay switched on.

days.label: Today
days.note: Tiny but clean with all-day offshores — a Monday for the south-exposed corners. Long Reef and Curl Curl hold the most at thigh–waist; Dee Why and Manly are knee-high dribble. Only 17kJ of energy and the dominant push is a S 10s swell that wraps poorly into Dee Why, so bring your floatiest board and keep expectations low. Your 10–12 slot rides the drop toward the 12:44pm low — mid-morning mid-tide is the call. 🦈 No shark detections at your spots, but drones evacuated Palm, Mona Vale & Warriewood yesterday — northern beaches are active.

swells.label: Primary — S groundswell (10kJ), wraps poorly here

dee-why.window: Mid-morning around mid-tide as it drops to the 12:44pm low. Small enough that it's a log-and-glide, not a performance session.

tip.label: Point call
tip.text: Only 17kJ at 60/100 and the energy is a dead-S 192° swell — that wraps badly into the Point (your classic S-swell fade). Forget the rights today; the beachie peaks with your highest-volume board are the play.

long-reef.window: The best size-holder of the four — the S-facing reef catches what little S swell there is. Mid-morning, mid-tide.

tip.label: Spot call
tip.text: 17kJ at 60/100 but Long Reef's S exposure squeezes out thigh–waist peelers. Grab it if you want the most rideable wall today — floaty board, clean offshore faces.

curl-curl.window: Another S-swell magnet holding thigh–waist. Mid-morning banks, offshore all session.

tip.label: Spot call
tip.text: Level with Long Reef at 17kJ — the southern corner banks bend the S swell in. Small and fun, empty and clean. Volume is your friend.

manly.window: Most sheltered of the four — near-flat. North Steyne might have a knee-high runner but it's marginal.

tip.label: Spot call
tip.text: 17kJ but Manly's north-end shelter kills most of the S swell — knee-high and soft. Skip it for Long Reef or Curl Curl today.

days.label: Tomorrow
days.note: Even smaller than Monday — down to just 5kJ as the last S traces fade, but still clean with breezy NW offshores. A rest day, or a longboard glide at Long Reef if you need a fix. Wednesday's southerly change brings wind and the first of the building swell.

dee-why.window: Near-flat — log it or skip it.

long-reef.window: Only spot with a chance of a rideable one — the reef milks the last of the S energy.

days.label: Weekend
days.note: The event of the week. A solid E groundswell fills in — Saturday builds through waist–chest to chest–head (164kJ), Sunday peaks at chest–head-plus with 280kJ and a long-period 17s S groundswell in the mix. Best of all, the E direction (86–94°) sits close to your favoured window and wraps cleanly into Dee Why — none of the dead-S fade. Sunday's the pick: bigger, and winds drop to near-glassy light ESE in the morning. Frontside rights at the Point should finally run.

dee-why.window: Sunday's the call — the E swell (86°) wraps into the Point where dead-S never does, and morning winds go light ESE. Get there for the 10am window; frontside rights should be running.

long-reef.window: Biggest of the four — Sunday could push head to a foot overhead as the E groundswell peaks at 280kJ. Watch the sets.

curl-curl.window: Punchy beach-break peaks both days, bigger Sunday — the performance option if you want open faces.

manly.window: Rideable both days and the most forgiving on the bigger Sunday — the protected pick if the swell overdelivers.
